titel: Additional budget adjustments for 2026 – Support for Ukraine and vaccine preparedness.
html:
In this proposal, the government presents proposed changes to the state budget for 2026. These proposals are primarily a consequence of the serious geopolitical situation in Sweden's vicinity and the deteriorating economic situation in Ukraine, due to Russia's full-scale invasion of Ukraine.

The proposal includes a provision authorizing the government to donate grenade launchers with ammunition to Ukraine, which the Swedish Armed Forces can temporarily dispense with. The proposal also includes suggestions for increased funding and authorizations to acquire, develop, and prepare equipment for donation to Ukraine, as well as to replace a portion of the equipment proposed to be donated to the country. Furthermore, the proposal includes suggestions for increased funding to support Ukraine in strengthening the country's total defense capabilities. The proposal also suggests that Sweden provide a guarantee for a loan from the International Bank for Reconstruction and Development to Ukraine.

The proposal also includes suggestions to ensure Sweden's preparedness for vaccinations during influenza pandemics.
tldr: The government wants to revise the 2026 budget to provide more support to Ukraine and to better prepare Sweden for future pandemics. This involves sending weapons and money to Ukraine, as well as ensuring access to vaccines.
lattlast:
The government has proposed an additional budget for the year 2026. The reason is the uncertain situation in Europe and the increasingly difficult war in Ukraine for the country. The proposal means that Sweden will be able to provide more assistance to Ukraine.

Sweden wants to send grenade launchers and ammunition to the Ukrainian army. To make this possible, the government wants to give the Swedish Armed Forces the authority to use resources to purchase new items that can replace what is being sent. This also involves providing more money to strengthen Ukraine's ability to defend itself.

Sweden also wants to provide economic assistance by guaranteeing a loan to Ukraine. This loan will come from a large international bank called the International Reconstruction and Development Bank. The purpose is to help the country rebuild.

In addition to the support for Ukraine, there is a proposal to improve Sweden's preparedness for vaccines. This means that Sweden should be better prepared to handle new pandemics, for example, if a serious influenza outbreak occurs in the future.
